folio.label: 105v
folio.l: ¢ This worthy Clerk , whan ended was his tale
Oure host seyde , and swor by goddes bones
Me were leuere , than a Barel of ale
My wyf at hom , had herd this legend ones
This is a gentil tale , for the nones
As to my purpos , wyst ©e my wylle
But thyngö that wol nat be , lat it be stille

note.notetxt: 7/1The incipit was written at the end of the folio ca. three lines before the bottom ruling line. The incipit is writte with a different cut of teh quilt. Note also that of the Marchau~t is in English whereas explicit and incipit is in Latin.ODR
1The stanza is accomodated in a blank space, defined by two prickings. One jthe end of L13 and onother one at the end of L14, both located in the left margin.ODR
